they focus on the code's static nature and standard pattern.\n   - No unwarranted assumptions are made.\n\n6. **Consistency of Scores with the Report Content:**\n   - The scores are consistent with the detailed analysis.\n   - No discrepancies are observed.\n\n---\n\n### Final Critical Assessment:\n\n- **Malware Score:** 0 — The code is a standard autogenerated contract interface factory with no malicious behavior.\n- **Obfuscated:** 0 — No obfuscation or unusual constructs are present.\n- **Risk Score:** 0 — No security risks are identified, given the code's static, read-only nature.\n\n### My Personal Estimation:\n\nGiven the detailed, consistent analysis across all reports, I concur with their assessments. The code is a benign, standard utility for smart contract interaction.\n\n**However,** considering the nature of supply chain risks, I would assign the following scores:\n\n- **Malware:** **0** — No malicious activity.\n- **Obfuscated:** **0** — Clear, standard code.\n- **Risk:** **0** — No security risk in static interface code.\n\n**Rationale:**  \nThe code does not process untrusted data, perform external calls beyond contract interaction, or contain any hidden or malicious logic.
